questions.question: List two advantages of using MPI derived datatypes over manual packing and unpacking of non-contiguous data.
expectedPoints: Improved performance: Allows MPI to optimize data gathering/scattering, potentially using hardware features.
Improved code clarity: Communication code becomes more readable and maintainable by abstracting buffer management.

questions.question: Explain the process of creating and using an MPI derived datatype to send a specific row of a matrix that is stored in row-major order in a 1D array. Include the necessary MPI function calls and their arguments.
expectedPoints: Identify that sending a row requires communicating a contiguous block of elements.
Use the type in a send: MPI_Send(&matrix[row_index*num_columns], 1, row_type, dest, tag, comm);
Mention that the start address is calculated based on the row index.
